verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 encodes a type II single-pass transmembrane glycoprotein. The long isoform (longer N-terminal cytoplasmic domain) is documented by primary literature (PubMed:1714903) and UniProt annotation to be 'mainly localized at the plasma membrane' as a single-pass type II membrane protein, with the catalytic domain exposed on the extracellular/luminal face. GeneCards/UniProt confirm 'Cell membrane; Single-pass type II membrane protein' and 'Cell surface' annotations. The TM anchor is retained (uncleaved), providing a direct, stable link to the outer leaflet. Multiple cell types show constitutive surface expression, making this a `yes` rather than merely contextual.
